fulltext: INTERNSHIP REPORT ON Data Analytics Submitted in the fulfilment of the requirement for the degree of Bachelor of Computer Applications By Arya N Rajavanthi(U03P A23S0068) Carried Out At “X-Ciencia Technologies India PVT. LTD” Under the Guidance of Internal Guide External Guide Kavya K.C Joel Fernandes Department of Computer Science THE NATIONAL COLLEGE Autonomous Jayanagar, Bangalore-560070 2025-2026 THE NATIONAL COLLEGE Autonomous Jayanagar, Bangalore560070 CERTIFICATE This is to certify the Internship report titled is a bonafide record of work done by Arya N Rajavanthi (U03PA23S0068) of THE NATIONAL COLLEGE, Jayanagar, Bengaluru, in partial fulfilment of the requirements of VI Semester BCA during the year 2025-2026. HEAD OF THE DEPARTMENT GUIDE Examiners: Examination Centre 1. The National College Autonomous 2. Jayanagar, Bangalore-70 COMPANY CERTIFICATE DECLARATION I hereby declare that the work presented in this report entitled “SUPPLY CHAIN AI ANALYST PIPELINE” in the fulfilment of the requirements for the award of the degree of Bachelor of Computer Applications submitted in the Department of Computer Science “The National College Jayanagar Bangalore” is an authentic record of my own work at Company “X-CIENCIA TECHNOLOGIES INDIA PVT.LTD”. Branch Office: 28th Main, near East End Circle, Jayanagar 9th Block, Bengaluru - 560069” carried out over a period from December 2025 to January 2026. The matter embodied in the report has not been submitted for the award of any other degree or diploma. ABSTRACT The Supply Chain AI Analyst Pipeline is an automated data processing solution designed to track and improve retail fulfillment reliability. The system utilizes n8n for server-side workflow automation, identifying daily sales emails and extracting JSON data from CSV attachments. This data is ingested into a cloudhosted PostgreSQL database on Supabase. Using the Quadratic AI spreadsheet, Python scripts were developed to perform complex data merging and cleaning. The project calculates vital supply chain KPIs, most notably On-Time In Full (OTIF), which measures the delivery performance of the organization. The automated pipeline successfully identified a 45.9% OTIF score, providing actionable insights into logistics bottlenecks. ACKNOWLEDGEMENT I take this opportunity to express my sincere thanks and deep gratitude to all those people who extended their wholehearted cooperation and have helped me in completing this internship successfully. First of all, I would like to thank Kavya KC, my mentor, who mentored me, guided me and challenged me. Last but not the least, I would like to thank the founders and the HR department of the organization for considering me a part of their team and providing such a great platform to learn and enhance my skills in Data Analytics. A very special thanks goes to all the faculties of the Computer Science Department under whose guidance I have been able to excel in my career and become a part of the X-CIENCIA TECHNOLOGIES INDIA PVT.LTD, Jayanagar 9th Block, Bangalore-560069 family. ARYA N RAJA V ANTHI (U03PA23S0068) TABLE OF CONTENT SLNO Content Page no. 1 INTRODUCTION 1 2 COMPANY OVERVIEW 2 3 INTERNSHIP OBJECTIVES 3 4 MAIN TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES 4 5 KEY PROJECTS 5 6 SKILLS ACQUIRED 6 7 CHALLENGES AND SOLUTIONS 7 8 PROJECT OVERVIEW 8 9 PROJECT OUTCOME 9-10 10 CONCLUSION 11 Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 1 INTRODUCTION Internship Duration: Dec 2025 – Jan 2026 Company: X-CIENCIA TECHNOLOGIES INDIA PVT.LTD. Branch Office: 28th Main, near East End Circle, Jayanagar 9th Block, Bengaluru – 560069 Department: Department of Computer Science Supervisor: Kavya KC (Internal) and Joel Fernandes(External) This report provides an overview of my internship experience at: X-CIENCIA TECHNOLOGIES INDIA PVT.LTD., highlighting the tasks and projects I was involved in—specifically the development of a Supply Chain AI Analyst Pipeline—the skills and knowledge I gained, and my overall reflections on the experience. X-CIENCIA TECHNOLOGIES INDIA PVT.LTD is a comprehensive information technology services and solutions provider, committed to delivering high-quality technology solutions that empower individuals and organizations under the mission of "Tech for People". With an integrated portfolio of products and services, serves a diverse customer base, specializing in custom software development, cloud solutions, and data engineering. As an ISO 9001:2015 certified organization, derives its strength from a visionary leadership team that fosters a culture of innovation and excellence. The company is strategically positioned at its branch office in Jayanagar 9th Block, Bengaluru, to serve businesses with cutting-edge technology solutions and robust data analytics services. Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 2 COMPANY OVERVIEW X-CIENCIA TECHNOLOGIES INDIA PVT.LTD is an ISO 9001:2015 certified IT services and consulting firm headquartered in Bangalore, India. Founded with the mission "Tech for People," the company specializes in delivering end-to-end technology solutions across various domains, including logistics, supply chain management, and data analytics. Services TSIPL offers a wide array of technology services: • Custom Software Development: Tailored solutions for enterprise applications and automated data pipelines. • Data Engineering & Business Intelligence: Data warehousing, real-time analytics, and automated reporting services using tools like PostgreSQL and AI-powered spreadsheets. • Cloud Solutions: Cloud migration and infrastructure setup using secure, scalable platforms like Supabase. • Workflow Automation: Designing end-to-end automation triggers (e.g., n8n) to optimize business operations and data ingestion. Strategic Vision TSIPL positions itself as a "trusted technology partner" for organizations undergoing digital transformation. Its mission is to "enable excellence through IT innovation and execution". The organization aims to continue expanding its reach in artificial intelligence, advanced data engineering, and cloud-native automation services. Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 3 INTERNSHIP OBJECTIVES The internship aims to allow students to fast track their careers. With the intention to design or consult, teach or project manage, the internship aims to deliver taught information, make explicit and practice the skills needed to be better equipped for these goals. Assessment of success in the internship is within the students themselves. We aim to make the learning opportunity a conscious one, to present PDC extension studies in a cohesive progression, offer activities using a variety of learning styles, revisit and reposition skills to solidify learning. Opportunities for assessment of progress made lie in the learning portfolio compiled by the student (or with a facilitator as note-taker) throughout the programme: to track aims, competence and confidence. Designs will be made up throughout the course, focusing on different circumstances with different aims for learning from the exercise, with guided peer review to broaden the learning opportunity in reflection. The internship should help the student understand data-driven design, to move towards teaching or consulting professionally. Taught elements are supported by practical involvement in the day to day running of the organization. Concurrent learning streams: • Taught elements — course extension learning on key elements of Data Analytics and Software design. • Practical elements — workshops for skills and methods used in areas of Cloud Databases and Automation taught elements. • Taught specialist courses — certificated independent courses covering key professional areas of expertise. • Interest elements — project work drawing on workshop skills, applied to the development of the Supply Chain AI Analyst Pipeline. • Reflective learning — self guided, with assistance of facilitators, to track and exhibit progress through the portfolio. Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 4 MAIN TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Data Engineering and Workflow Automation: • Configuring n8n triggers to automatically monitor Gmail for incoming "daily sales" CSV attachments. • Implementing automated extraction logic to parse raw CSV files and aggregate data into standardized JSON formats. • Designing and maintaining ETL pipelines for seamless automated ingestion of logistical data into cloud-based storage. 2. Database Management: • Designing a Star Schema architecture in a cloud-hosted PostgreSQL environment via Supabase. • Managing relational tables, including dimension tables like dim_products and fact tables like fact_order_line. • Implementing secure API authentication mechanisms using Google OAuth 2.0 client credentials. 3. AI-Powered Analytics and KPI Calculation: • Writing Python scripts within Quadratic for automated data cleaning, whitespace removal, and ID standardization. • Implementing complex logistical KPI formulas such as OTIF (On-Time In Full) and Volume Fill Rate. • Utilizing natural language prompts to generate automated SQL and Python logic for supply chain insights. 4. Learning and Development: • Staying updated with the latest trends in agentic workflows and AI-integrated spreadsheet technologies. • Troubleshooting and debugging complex cloud connectivity and authentication errors (e.g., Error 403: access_denied). • Working on end-to-end project modules to build professional-grade data analytics portfolios. Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 5 KEY PROJECTS 1. Automated Data Ingestion (The Pipeline) The "brain" of the project is the n8n automation server, which handles the transition of data from source to storage without manual intervention. • Trigger: A Gmail node monitors for incoming emails titled "daily sales" containing CSV attachments. • Processing: The system parses the CSV files and aggregates the data into a standardized JSON format. • Storage: Validated data is automatically inserted into a cloud-hosted PostgreSQL database. 2. Cloud Database Architecture The data is stored securely in Supabase, utilizing a structured relational schema for optimized querying.  Schema Design: The project uses a Star Schema, organizing data into Dimension Tables (such as dim_products and dim_customers) and Fact Tables (such as fact_order_line).  Security: Authentication is managed via OAuth 2.0 client credentials generated through the Google Cloud Platform. 3. AI-Powered Analytics & Visualization For the final layer of analysis, the database is connected to the Quadratic AI spreadsheet. • Data Transformation: Python scripts within Quadratic are used to load, clean, and merge disparate tables from Supabase. • AI Integration: Natural language prompts are used to generate SQL queries and Python code for complex calculations, such as merging currency rates or identifying sales trends. Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 6 SKILLS ACQUIRED Technical Skills: • Data Engineering & Automation: o Developed skills in creating automated ETL pipelines using n8n to monitor emails and extract data. o Learned to handle JSON data structures and standardize them for cloud ingestion.  Cloud Database Management: o Gained expertise in managing PostgreSQL databases via the Supabase platform. o Understand how to design a Star Schema with relational fact and dimension tables. • AI-Powered Analytics: o Mastered the use of Quadratic AI to integrate backend database logic with spreadsheet- based analytics. o Applied Python programming for automated data cleaning, merging, and complex KPI calculations. Soft Skills: • Problem-Solving: o Developed critical thinking abilities by troubleshooting OAuth 2.0 authentication and API connectivity errors. • Collaboration: o Gained experience working within a technical team at Techmiya Solutions, aligning technical workflows with business goals. • Communication: o Improved the ability to communicate technical concepts, such as OTIF metrics, clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. • Time Management: o Learned to prioritize tasks and meet project milestones within a strict 30-day internship duration. Practical Experience: • Project Development: o Worked on a real-world Supply Chain AI Analyst project, gaining hands-on experience in the full software development lifecycle. • Cloud Security: o Participated in setting up secure access controls and verified test users within the Google Cloud Console. Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 7 CHALLENGES AND SOLUTIONS 1. API Authentication and Security: • Challenge: Encountering "Error 403: access_denied" while configuring Google OAuth 2.0 client credentials for Gmail integration. • Solution: o Configured the OAuth consent screen in the Google Cloud Console to add specific verified test users. o Ensured all necessary scopes were enabled to allow n8n to read email attachments securely. 2. Data Standardization and Cleaning: • Challenge: Raw CSV files delivered via email contained trailing whitespaces and inconsistent date formats that hindered database ingestion. • Solution: o Developed Python cleaning scripts to strip whitespace from product and customer IDs. o Implemented automated date conversion logic to ensure all transactional records adhered to the PostgreSQL standard. 3. Cloud Database Connectivity: • Challenge: Troubleshooting "Connection Refused" errors and hidden UI elements when linking the Quadratic spreadsheet to the Supabase database. • Solution: o Performed a manual database password reset within the Supabase settings to refresh the connection string. o Utilized browser zoom adjustments to reveal hidden configuration panes in the cloud dashboard. 4. Meeting Deadlines and Managing Expectations: • Challenge: Balancing the construction of an end-to-end automation pipeline with foundational learning within a strict 30-day internship duration.  Solution: o Broke down the project into distinct phases: Orientation, Foundation, Automation, and Analytics. o Prioritized tasks based on their impact on the final OTIF (On-Time In Full) dashboard goals. 5. System Integration Reliability: • Challenge: Ensuring seamless data flow between Gmail, n8n, Supabase, and Quadratic without manual intervention. • Solution: o Regularly tested interactions between front-end spreadsheet cells and back-end database tables to catch integration bugs early Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 8 PROJECT OVERVIEW TECHNOLOGIES USED n8n: n8n is an extendable workflow automation tool that enables the connection of various applications and APIs to automate repetitive tasks. In this project, it serves as the central automation engine that monitors Gmail for incoming sales data and extracts CSV attachments. Supabase (PostgreSQL): Supabase is an open-source platform that provides a suite of backend services, primarily built around a cloud-hosted PostgreSQL relational database. It is utilized for storing structured logistical data, including product dimensions, customer details, and transactional order lines. Python: Python is a powerful, modern, and high-level programming language known for its extensive libraries in data science and automation. It is primarily used within the analytics layer to clean raw datasets, merge relational tables, and implement the logic for critical performance indicators like On-Time In Full (OTIF). Quadratic: Quadratic is an AI-powered spreadsheet platform that bridges the gap between traditional spreadsheets and modern data science. It allows for the execution of Python code directly within grid cells, enabling the project to pull real-time data from the cloud database and visualize complex supply chain metrics. Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 9 PROJECT OUTCOMES This n8n automation workflow serves as an agentic trigger that monitors Gmail for sales data and extracts CSV attachmentsThe extracted records are then parsed and automatically inserted into a cloud-hosted PostgreSQL database for structured storage and real-time analytics. This image displays the Supabase Table Editor showing the dim_products dimension table within your cloud-hosted PostgreSQL database.It illustrates the structured storage of product metadata, such as IDs and pricing, as part of the Star Schema architecture used for your supply chain analytics. Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 10 This Quadratic AI interface demonstrates the use of Python scripting to automate the loading, cleaning, and merging of disparate supply chain datasets.The script standardizes transactional data by stripping whitespaces and converting IDs to integers to ensure accuracy in the final analytics summary. This Quadratic AI dashboard features a "KPI Summary" bar chart that visualizes critical performance metrics such as Line Fill Rate and On Time In Full %.It serves as the final reporting layer of the pipeline, providing stakeholders with an immediate, data-driven comparison of percentage-based logistical indicators. Internship Report The National College Jayanagar 11 CONCLUSION During my internship in data analytics, I have had the opportunity to delve deep into the realm of Python programming and its applications in building automated data pipelines. This experience has been invaluable, providing me with both technical skills and practical insights that will shape my future career in data science and business intelligence. Reflecting on my internship at: X-CIENCIA TECHNOLOGIES INDIA PVT.LTD., I have traversed a transformative journey of learning and growth. From mastering Python programming and automation platforms like n8n, to crafting real-time supply chain dashboards in Quadratic, every challenge has been a stepping stone towards enhancing my technical proficiency. Debugging automated data ingestion workflows, optimizing logistical KPI calculations such as OTIF (On-Time In Full), and integrating cloud-based database systems like Supabase have refined my problemsolving acumen and fortified my commitment to delivering robust, data-driven solutions.
